120-Day Transaction Calendar
ESOP Acquisition Timeline for {{companyName}}

LOI Signing

§ 01Phase 1 — Mobilization
Weeks 1–2: Engagement & Team AssemblyDay 1–14
Wk 1Engagement letter signed; ERISA counsel retained; independent trustee selected; project kickoff callForhemit
Wk 2Initial document request issued; seller financials collected; QofE firm engaged; lender shortlist draftedForhemit
Weeks 3–4: Preliminary Valuation & Financial ReviewDay 15–28
Wk 3Management presentations; site visit; preliminary valuation analysis; initial capital stack modelingForhemit + Seller
Wk 4QofE draft received; preliminary valuation range confirmed; lender outreach initiatedForhemit + Appraiser
◆Gate 1: Valuation confirmed; proceed to formal diligence{{gate1Date}}
§ 02Phase 2 — Diligence
Weeks 5–6: Financial & Legal DiligenceDay 29–42
Wk 5Financial model finalized; tax structure analysis complete; ERISA counsel review of plan documents beginsForhemit + Counsel
Wk 6Lender term sheets received and compared; collateral analysis; insurance review; HR/employee census verifiedForhemit + Lenders
Weeks 7–8: Lender Selection & Plan DesignDay 43–56
Wk 7Lender selected; commitment letter executed; ESOP plan document drafted; trustee review of plan termsForhemit + Lender
Wk 8Repurchase obligation study initiated; employee communication plan drafted; DOL advisory opinion requested if neededForhemit + Trustee
◆Gate 2: Lender committed; plan design approved by trustee{{gate2Date}}
§ 03Phase 3 — Documentation
Weeks 9–10: Transaction DocumentsDay 57–70
Wk 9Stock purchase agreement drafted; seller representations and warranties negotiated; escrow terms finalizedCounsel + Forhemit
Wk 10Loan documents drafted; security agreements; guarantees; intercreditor agreements (if applicable)Lender + Counsel
Weeks 11–12: Regulatory & Final ApprovalsDay 71–84
Wk 11Final appraisal delivered; IRS determination letter application filed (if needed); insurance binder confirmedAppraiser + Counsel
Wk 12All closing conditions reviewed; outstanding diligence items resolved; pre-closing checklist completedForhemit
◆Gate 3: All documents finalized; closing conditions satisfied{{gate3Date}}
§ 04Phase 4 — Closing & Transition
Week 13: Pre-ClosingDay 85–98
Wk 13Final closing checklist executed; funds flow memo circulated; all parties confirm readiness; employee communication materials finalizedForhemit + All Parties
Weeks 14–15: ClosingDay 99–112
Wk 14Closing: stock purchased by ESOP trust; loan funded; seller proceeds wired; escrow releasedAll Parties
★CLOSING{{closingDate}}
Wk 15Post-closing filings; employee announcement; transition plan activated; 1042 election deadline trackedForhemit + Seller
Weeks 16–17: Post-Close StabilizationDay 113–120+
Wk 16First trustee meeting; repurchase obligation monitoring initiated; accounting transition to ESOP reportingTrustee + Forhemit
Wk 1790-day check-in scheduled; ongoing governance framework established; annual valuation process documentedForhemit
§ 05Critical Path & Dependencies

The following items are on the critical path. Delays in any of these will push the closing date.

Critical path items: (1) Timely delivery of seller financials and tax returns, (2) QofE completion within the first 4 weeks, (3) Lender term sheet by week 6, (4) Trustee approval of plan documents by week 8, (5) Final appraisal delivery by week 11.
External dependencies: IRS determination letter timelines (if required), DOL advisory opinion (if requested), third-party consents and regulatory approvals specific to {{companyName}}'s industry.
—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
California Public Benefit Corporation
Project Lead
